description:
Substrate for VR.4: classify each fn as either parking-reachable (transitively reaches `Term::Receive` or calls a closure / closure-reachable target) or non-parking. Direct callers of non-parking fns can later switch to a native typed ABI (.6.2+) while parking-reachable fns keep the heap-frame ABI (parking requires heap-resident state for the scheduler to suspend/resume).

## Algorithm

  parking_reachable := { f : f.body contains Term::Receive }
  // closure invocations are opaque — conservatively treat reachable.
  union closure_reachable_callees(m)
  fixed-point: parking_reachable += { f : f's body has Term::Call/TailCall/CallClosure/TailCallClosure to anything already in parking_reachable }

One pass usually converges; iterate to be safe.

## Adds

- `ir_typer::parking_reachable(m: &Module) -> HashSet<FnId>` or a new `src/parking.rs` module. Returns the set.
- Unit tests: a `receive_loop` fn is in the set; a pure helper fn is not; a fn that calls `receive_loop` directly is in the set; a fn that builds a closure pointing at `receive_loop` puts both in the set; a fn that calls a closure (any closure) is in the set.

## Kills

- Nothing yet — this is pure utility.

## Acceptance

- The analysis returns a sound over-approximation: every fn that can possibly park is in the set. False positives are acceptable (a fn that's marked parking-reachable but never actually parks pays a small perf cost — heap frame allocation — but is correct).
- cargo test green.

## Blocks

- fz-ul4.27.6.2 (typed sigs + native direct calls)
title: VR.4.1: parking-reachability analysis
